generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only identifiers (features, constants) visible in the AADL model when constructing AGREE contracts.
Ensure helper declarations (eq, const) have correct types (int, real, bool) matching their expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` to define helper expressions and `const` to define named constants.
Range checks typically use a boolean helper: `eq range_ok : bool = x >= lower and x <= upper;`
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers (types, features) in AGREE that are not present in the AADL model.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within implementation annexes; use assignments instead.
Do not attempt to fix type mismatches by modifying the AADL model; adjust the AGREE helper declaration.
